violations.message: API contact information is recommended for AI readiness
violations.description: API should have contact information for AI agent queries
violations.fixSuggestion: Add `info.contact` with at least an email or URL so humans and tools can report issues or request access.

violations.message: Path item should include a description for better AI route understanding
violations.description: Path items should have descriptions to provide route-level context
violations.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` on the path item for shared behavior, pagination, or resource lifecycle across methods.

violations.message: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
violations.description: Path items should have summaries to provide route-level context
violations.fixSuggestion: Set `summary` on the path item to describe the resource or route group before drilling into per-method details.

violations.message: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets
violations.description: GET operations should support standard pagination parameters
violations.fixSuggestion: Add query parameters such as `limit` plus `cursor` or `page` (and document defaults and max) on list-style GETs.

violations.message: Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization
violations.description: All operations should have tags for AI categorization
violations.fixSuggestion: Add `tags` that reference your global `tags` list so operations group correctly in docs and AI context windows.

violations.message: Operation must define at least one 4xx error response for AI error handling
violations.fixSuggestion: Document the client and auth failure cases you support (e.g. 400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 429) with bodies agents can parse.

violations.message: Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling
violations.fixSuggestion: Add a `500` (or `502`/`503`/`504` as appropriate) response with a schema for generic or upstream failures.

violations.message: Response should have an example for AI understanding
violations.fixSuggestion: Under each 2xx response media type, add `example` or `examples` for a representative success payload.

violations.message: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating
violations.description: List endpoint responses must include pagination metadata
violations.fixSuggestion: Expose at least one of `has_more`, `next_cursor`/`next_page`, or `total` in the JSON body schema for paginated lists.

passedRules.message: API description is required and should be at least 50 characters for AI readiness
passedRules.description: API must have a comprehensive description for AI understanding
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Expand `info.description` to at least 50 characters: scope, main resources, auth model, and links to external docs or portals.

passedRules.message: Server should have a description (e.g., production, staging) for AI context
passedRules.description: Server entries should have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: On each `servers[]` entry, set `description` to the environment and audience (e.g. "Production, customer-facing, EU only").

passedRules.message: Operation '{{property}}' must have a summary for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All operations must have a summary for AI understanding
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a one-line `summary` for each operation (what it does, not how it is implemented) for list views and AI summarization.

passedRules.message: Callback operation '{{property}}' should have a summary for AI readiness
passedRules.description: Callback operations should have summaries for AI understanding
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `summary` to each operation under `callbacks` so event-driven flows are self-explanatory in documentation.

passedRules.message: Webhook operation '{{property}}' should have a summary for AI readiness
passedRules.description: Webhook operations should have summaries for AI understanding
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `summary` to each `webhooks` operation describing the event and payload purpose.

passedRules.message: Operation '{{property}}' must have a description (min 30 characters) for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All operations must have a detailed description
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Write at least 30 characters in `description`: inputs, outcomes, idempotency, and notable errors or side effects.

passedRules.message: Operation must have an operationId for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All operations must have an operationId for AI reference
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set a unique `operationId` per operation for stable cross-references in code generators and agent prompts.

passedRules.message: operationId style should be consistent across operations for reliable AI references
passedRules.description: operationIds should use a consistent naming style across the API
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Pick one style (camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case, or PascalCase) and rename `operationId` values to match across the spec.

passedRules.message: operationId values must be unique across the API for reliable AI references
passedRules.description: operationIds must be unique across all operations
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Rename duplicate `operationId` values so each operation has one unique identifier across paths and methods.

passedRules.message: Parameter '{{value.name}}' must have a description for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All parameters must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Document each parameter: meaning, format, default, and when it is required.

passedRules.message: Parameter '{{value.name}}' description should be at least 15 characters
passedRules.description: Parameter descriptions should be meaningful
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Extend the description beyond 15 characters with units, valid ranges, or example values.

passedRules.message: Parameter '{{value.name}}' should have an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Query and path parameters should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `example` or `examples` on the parameter so agents can send realistic values.

passedRules.message: Path-level parameter '{{value.name}}' should have an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Path-level parameters should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: On path-level `parameters`, add an `example` matching the path template segment.

passedRules.message: Parameter '{{value.name}}' content should include an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Parameters using content should include examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Under `content` media types, add `example` or `examples` for the serialized parameter value.

passedRules.message: Path-level parameter content should include an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Path-level parameters using content should include examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For path parameters defined with `content`, add a concrete `example` under each media type.

passedRules.message: Request body must have a description for AI readiness
passedRules.description: Request bodies must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Describe `requestBody` purpose, required fields, and semantic meaning for each `POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH` operation.

passedRules.message: Request body for {{property}} should have an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Request bodies should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add an `example` (or `examples`) under the request body’s media type content so agents can mirror a valid JSON payload.

passedRules.message: Request body schema should have an explicit type so AI agents construct valid payloads
passedRules.description: Request body schemas must have explicit types
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set `type` (or use `allOf`/`oneOf` with typed branches) on the request body schema root and nested objects where applicable.

passedRules.message: Request body schema should declare a 'required' array to clarify mandatory fields for AI agents
passedRules.description: Request body schemas should define a required array
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `required: [...]` listing mandatory property names for the request object schema.

passedRules.message: Response '{{property}}' must have a description for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All responses must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` to each response code explaining when it is returned and what the body represents.

passedRules.message: Response header '{{property}}' should include an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Response headers should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `example` on each documented response header (e.g. ETag, RateLimit-*) so tools know the expected format.

passedRules.message: Response should specify content type (e.g., application/json) for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Responses should specify content types
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `content` map with at least one media type key (typically `application/json`) for each success response.

passedRules.message: Response schema should have an explicit type so AI agents can parse response payloads
passedRules.description: Response schemas must have explicit types
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Ensure each response `schema` has explicit `type` or a composition of typed schemas (not an empty object).

passedRules.message: 2xx response '{{property}}' must define response content so AI agents can reliably parse success payloads
passedRules.description: All success responses must define structured payload content
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For each 2xx response, add `content` with a media type and body schema (or empty object schema for 204 if applicable).

passedRules.message: 2xx response '{{property}}' application/json content must define a schema for consistent AI consumption
passedRules.description: JSON success responses must define a schema
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Under `content['application/json']`, add a `schema` (or $ref) describing the success JSON body.

passedRules.message: Error response '{{property}}' must define response content so AI agents can parse failures consistently
passedRules.description: All error responses must define structured payload content
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For 4xx/5xx responses, add `content` with at least one media type so errors are not undocumented plain text.

passedRules.message: Error response '{{property}}' application/json content must define a schema for consistent AI error handling
passedRules.description: JSON error responses must define a schema
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Under `content['application/json']` for error statuses, define a `schema` for your standard error envelope (code, message, details).

passedRules.message: Operation must define at least one success response (2xx) for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Operations must have success responses
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add at least one of `200`, `201`, `202`, or `204` under `responses` with documented content where a body is returned.

passedRules.message: Error response should have a schema definition for AI error handling
passedRules.description: Error responses should have proper schemas
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For each 4xx/5xx `content` entry, add a `schema` (or $ref to a shared Error model) so failures are structured.

passedRules.message: Error response '{{property}}' description should be at least 20 characters so AI agents can understand failure semantics
passedRules.description: Error responses should include meaningful descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Expand the response `description` to explain when this status is used and what the client should do next (retry, fix input, re-auth).

passedRules.message: Schema '{{property}}' must have a description for AI readiness
passedRules.description: All schemas must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `description` to each component schema summarizing the object’s role and key fields.

passedRules.message: Schema '{{property}}' description should be at least 20 characters
passedRules.description: Schema descriptions should be meaningful
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Elaborate the schema `description` with field semantics, invariants, and relationships to other models.

passedRules.message: Schema '{{property}}' should have a title to help AI agents identify its semantic meaning
passedRules.description: Component schemas should have titles for AI discoverability
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set `title` to a short human-readable name (e.g. "UserAccount") used in generated docs and prompts.

passedRules.message: Schema '{{property}}' must have an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: All schemas must have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `example` or `examples` at the schema level that validates against the same schema.

passedRules.message: Property '{{property}}' in schema must have a description
passedRules.description: Schema properties must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Document every `properties` entry: meaning, nullability, and how it relates to other fields.

passedRules.message: Property '{{property}}' should have an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Schema properties should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `example` (or media-specific examples) for non-trivial properties so agents pick valid values.

passedRules.message: Header '{{property}}' should include an example for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Component headers should have examples
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `example` on reusable `components.headers` entries matching the declared schema.

passedRules.message: Enum property '{{property}}' should have a description explaining possible values
passedRules.description: Enum values should have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Describe what each enum value means, or use `x-` extensions / oneOf with titled variants if values are not self-explanatory.

passedRules.message: Schema property '{{property}}' must have an explicit type defined to prevent ambiguous AI interpretation
passedRules.description: All schema properties must have an explicit type
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set `type` on each property, or use `allOf`/`oneOf` with unambiguous branches—avoid leaving properties untyped.

passedRules.message: Parameter schema at '{{property}}' must have an explicit type
passedRules.description: All parameter schemas must have explicit types
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `type` (or $ref) to the parameter’s `schema` so query/path/header values are unambiguous.

passedRules.message: String property '{{property}}' should use a format modifier (e.g., date-time, email, uuid) for unambiguous AI interpretation
passedRules.description: String properties with semantic meaning should use format modifiers
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `format` for strings that are dates, emails, UUIDs, URIs, etc., per OpenAPI/JSON Schema conventions.

passedRules.message: Schema '{{property}}' is typed as 'object' but declares no properties — define properties, additionalProperties, or a composition keyword
passedRules.description: Object schemas must define their structure — empty type:object is ambiguous
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `properties`, a typed `additionalProperties: false` object, or `allOf`/`oneOf` so the object shape is known to agents.

passedRules.message: Property '{{property}}' is typed as 'object' but declares no properties — empty object types prevent AI agents from constructing valid payloads
passedRules.description: Object-typed properties must define their structure
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Inline a defined sub-object, `$ref` to a component schema, or add `properties` / composition keywords under that property.

passedRules.message: Array schema '{{property}}' must define an 'items' schema so AI agents understand the element type
passedRules.description: Array schemas must define their item type
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set `items` to a concrete schema (primitive, object, or $ref) for every `type: array` model.

passedRules.message: Array property '{{property}}' must define an 'items' schema — untyped arrays are ambiguous for AI agents
passedRules.description: Array-type properties must define their item schema
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For array-valued properties, define `items` to describe the element type and constraints.

passedRules.message: Schema property '{{property}}' should define constraints (pattern/minLength/maxLength/minimum/maximum) to guide deterministic AI payload generation
passedRules.description: Scalar schema properties should define validation constraints
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add at least one constraint: `minLength`/`maxLength`/`pattern` for strings, `minimum`/`maximum` for numbers, or `enum` for closed sets.

passedRules.message: Error schema must define 'message' and 'code' properties so AI agents can interpret and correct failed requests
passedRules.description: Error response schemas must contain structured fields for AI self-correction
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `message` (human-readable) and `code` (stable machine id) to your error model, or $ref a shared error component that includes them.

passedRules.message: Error schema should include 'type', 'title', and 'detail' fields per RFC 7807 for rich AI error semantics
passedRules.description: Error responses should follow RFC 7807 problem detail structure
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Model errors as Problem Details: `type` (URI), `title`, `detail`, optional `instance`—or $ref a component schema that matches RFC 7807.

passedRules.message: Error schema should include a 'details' field so AI agents receive contextual information about what failed
passedRules.description: Error schemas should include a details field for additional context
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a `details` (or `errors` array) field for field-level or validation context—especially for 4xx responses.

passedRules.message: 422 error schema should include both 'expected' and 'received' fields so AI agents can automatically correct malformed requests
passedRules.description: Validation error schemas should carry expected and received context for agent self-correction
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For 422 bodies, include `expected` (schema or rule) and `received` (submitted value) so agents can fix requests without guessing.

passedRules.message: 429 response must declare Retry-After or X-RateLimit-* headers so AI agents can back off and retry safely
passedRules.description: Rate-limited responses must include retry guidance headers
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Document `Retry-After` and/or `X-RateLimit-Limit` / `X-RateLimit-Remaining` / `X-RateLimit-Reset` on the 429 response (or link to a header component).

passedRules.message: Security scheme '{{property}}' uses an interactive OAuth flow (implicit/authorizationCode) — AI agents cannot complete browser-based authentication
passedRules.description: Security schemes must not use interactive OAuth flows
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Prefer client credentials, API keys, or pre-provisioned tokens; document how non-interactive clients obtain credentials.

passedRules.message: API should define security schemes in components for AI readiness
passedRules.description: API should define security schemes for AI understanding
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `components.securitySchemes` (e.g. `http` bearer, `apiKey`, `oauth2` clientCredentials) and reference them from `security` where needed.

passedRules.message: Security scheme '{{property}}' should have a description
passedRules.description: Security schemes should have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Describe how to obtain the credential, which header/query it uses, and any scopes or roles.

passedRules.message: Operation does not declare security requirements — AI agents need explicit security constraints to avoid unintended state changes
passedRules.description: Mutating operations should declare explicit security requirements
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Set `security` on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (or inherit from root `security`) so it is clear which auth applies when mutating state.

passedRules.message: POST/PATCH operation should declare an Idempotency-Key header parameter to prevent duplicate side-effects on AI agent retries
passedRules.description: Mutating operations should support idempotency for safe AI agent retries
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add a header parameter `Idempotency-Key` (string) and document server behavior: same key → same effective outcome, safe retries.

passedRules.message: Tag '{{value.name}}' must have a description for AI categorization
passedRules.description: All tags must have descriptions
passedRules.fixSuggestion: For each entry in the global `tags` array, add a `description` of what resources or workflows the tag groups.

passedRules.message: Tag '{{value.name}}' should have external documentation for AI learning
passedRules.description: Tags should have external documentation links
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `externalDocs` with a URL to deeper docs, tutorials, or domain context for that tag.

passedRules.message: Schema with oneOf/anyOf should define a discriminator for AI understanding
passedRules.description: Polymorphic schemas should use discriminators
passedRules.fixSuggestion: Add `discriminator` with `propertyName` and optional `mapping` to tie variants to a stable property value.

passedRules.message: Deprecated operation should have a detailed description explaining alternatives
passedRules.description: Deprecated operations should have clear notices
passedRules.fixSuggestion: In the operation `description`, state deprecation timeline, the replacement path or version, and migration steps with keywords like “deprecated” or “instead”.

dimensions.label: Semantic Discovery
dimensions.description: Can AI agents find the right endpoint and understand its intent?
dimensions.whyItMatters: AI agents rely on summaries, descriptions, and stable operation identifiers to select the right tool for a task. Without them, agents must infer behavior through trial and error, leading to incorrect calls and hallucinated responses.

missing.message: Path item should include a summary for better AI route understanding
Path item should include a description for better AI route understanding
Operation should have at least one tag for AI categorization

dimensions.label: Contract Integrity
dimensions.description: Can AI agents construct valid requests and interpret responses without guessing?
dimensions.whyItMatters: Agents generate payloads based on schemas and examples. Ambiguous types, missing required fields, or absent examples cause agents to produce invalid requests or misinterpret response data.

missing.message: Response should have an example for AI understanding
Operation must define at least one 4xx error response for AI error handling
Operation should define at least one 5xx error response for AI error handling

dimensions.label: Resilience & Recovery
dimensions.description: Can AI agents handle failures, rate limits, and large datasets gracefully?
dimensions.whyItMatters: Autonomous agents operate without human supervision. Structured error schemas let agents self-correct, rate limit headers prevent hammering, and pagination metadata tells agents when to stop iterating.

missing.message: GET operation should declare pagination parameters (cursor/page and limit) for AI agent iteration over large result sets

missing.displayPath: paths > /health > get > responses > 200 > content > application/json > schema
missing.message: GET list operation response schema should include pagination metadata (has_more, next_cursor, or total) so AI agents know when to stop paginating

dimensions.label: Security & Integrity
dimensions.description: Is the API safe for autonomous agent access over the long term?
dimensions.whyItMatters: Agents cannot complete interactive browser-based OAuth flows. Undefined security requirements risk agents making unintended state changes. Idempotency support prevents duplicate side-effects when agents retry operations.
